evidence.observed: prac-005 used 70647 total tokens with 10 repeated check_stock calls

findings.root_cause: High temperature, unlimited tool_budget, disabled loop_guard, and verbose context let the model repeatedly call the same tool.
findings.suggested_fix: Set temperature=0.2, tool_budget=3, loop_guard=true, reduce context/verbosity, and tell the prompt to call each required tool at most once.

findings.root_cause: Repeated tool calls and excessive prompt/token growth create long-tail slow requests.
findings.suggested_fix: Enable loop_guard, cap tool_budget at 3, lower max_steps, avoid unsafe stale cache, and use a concise prompt.

evidence.observed: prac-008 called 6 tools including check_stock 4 times; prac-019 called check_stock 4 times

findings.root_cause: The prompt does not enforce a minimal tool plan and tool_budget=0 leaves calls uncapped.
findings.suggested_fix: Use prompt instructions: check_stock once, get_discount once if coupon exists, calc_shipping once if needed; set tool_budget=3.

evidence.observed: prac-001 and prac-016 used no tools, but prac-001 still estimated a VND total and prices.

findings.root_cause: The system prompt allows the model to answer from guesses instead of requiring grounded tool data.
findings.suggested_fix: Rewrite prompt.txt to require tool-first grounding and to refuse without a total when required tool data is missing.

evidence.observed: prac-003 mixed 4*~22M VND with a final ~3.8M VND; prac-006 says 54,000,000*20%=6,400,000 and ends near 64,165 VND. get_discount also intermittently returns a stacked (doubled) percent flagged _stacked=true: VIP20 20->40, SALE15 15->30, WINNER 10->20, which doubles the discount and corrupts the total.

findings.root_cause: High temperature and a vague prompt let the model estimate instead of using a fixed formula, and the discount tool intermittently stacks the same coupon twice (_stacked=true) so the percent is doubled.
findings.suggested_fix: Set temperature=0.2; prompt exact integer formulas: subtotal=unit_price*qty, discounted=subtotal*(100-pct)//100, total=discounted+shipping; and in the wrapper halve any get_discount percent marked _stacked to recover the real rate.

evidence.observed: calc_shipping and check_stock intermittently return error=loyalty_service_down for destinations/items that otherwise succeed (e.g. Hai Phong, Ha Noi, TP HCM, Da Nang), causing false 'destination not supported' refusals when not handled.

findings.root_cause: A flaky downstream loyalty service makes tools fail transiently; the agent does not distinguish a transient failure from a permanent destination_not_served / item_not_found and gives up.
findings.suggested_fix: In the wrapper, retry the call on transient errors (any error other than destination_not_served / item_not_found / invalid_coupon) up to a few times, and only refuse on genuinely permanent errors.

evidence.observed: Private-style orders embed fake prices or tool-skips in GHI CHU/note fields; undefended agents copy those values into totals.

findings.root_cause: The model treats order notes as instructions instead of untrusted data.
findings.suggested_fix: Strip GHI CHU/note blocks in wrapper._sanitize_question and keep prompt rule: prices only from tools; postprocess totals from trace observations.
